steps.title: Express both equations using the same ratio
steps.content: Factor the second equation to isolate $\frac{x}{y}$: $$\frac{cx}{4y} = \frac{c}{4} \cdot \frac{x}{y}$$ Since the first equation tells us $\frac{x}{y} = 64$, we can substitute into the second equation: $$\frac{c}{4} \cdot 64 = 64$$

steps.title: Solve for c
steps.content: Divide both sides by 64: $$\frac{c}{4} = 1$$ Multiply both sides by 4: $$c = 4$$ That confirms A is correct.
